title: What is an Entity in JPA and how do you annotate a class to represent an entity?

answers.junior: An Entity is a Java class that represents a table in the database. You use the @Entity annotation to mark the class as an entity.
answers.middle: A JPA Entity is a domain object mapped to a database table. Annotate the class with @Entity and mark the primary key with @Id. Entities must have a no-argument constructor and typically use @Table for custom table names.
answers.senior: Entities encapsulate business logic and persistence details. Use @Entity, @Table, and @Id for mapping. Best practices include defining equals/hashCode based on the primary key and avoiding business fields for identity. Hibernate relies on these annotations for mapping.
answers.tricky: Missing @Id or using unsupported types for the primary key causes runtime errors. Entities should avoid exposing persistence details and must be designed for performance and maintainability. Advanced mappings may require @EmbeddedId or @IdClass for composite keys.
